nodes.name: 秘鲁
nodes.summary: 秘鲁是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联143种鸟类，包括北山䳍、高山斑䳍、丽色斑䳍等143种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: CR
nodes.summary: CR表示 IUCN 极危等级，当前关联56种鸟类，如勺嘴鹬、加州神鹫、菲律宾鹰等56种鸟类。

nodes.name: 凤冠雉科
nodes.summary: 凤冠雉科（Cracidae）是图谱中的科级分类节点，当前关联56种鸟类，如山冠雉、Black guan、Sickle-winged guan等56种鸟类。

nodes.name: El Sira Communal Reserve
nodes.summary: El Sira Communal Reserve是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括Sira curassow，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸡形目
nodes.summary: 鸡形目（Galliformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联298种鸟类，如眼斑冢雉、Red-billed brushturkey、Black-billed brushturkey等298种鸟类。

nodes.name: Sira curassow
nodes.summary: Sira curassow（Sira curassow，学名 Pauxi koepckeae），分类上属于鸡形目，并归入凤冠雉科，在图谱中标记为极危（CR）物种，已关联2个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in the Cerros del Sira in central Peru

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It is found in the Cerros del Sira in central Peru

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The Sira curassow is listed as critically endangered by BirdLife International for the IUCN as they believe it is threatened by habitat destruction and is hunted for meat

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The birds population is believed by BirdLife International in 2016 to be below 250 individuals, citing the 2014 IUCN assessment by Gastañaga for BirdLife International (no longer available online). In the 2016 IUCN assessment by BirdLife International Gastañaga in her 2011 study is said to have estimated the population to be 400. This is not actually stated in her report, instead it is estimated that outside of breeding season the birds occur at a density of less than one bird per over an area encompassing at least the four known areas of occurrence, within of each other (thus a minimum of , thus maximum of 900 birds). The 2016 IUCN assessment estimates that the extent of occurrence is (apparently all within the El Sira Communal Reserve), although it is unclear where this number comes from. Because of the difference between the stated populations in 2011 and 2014, the 2016 IUCN assessment assumes the population is experiencing a 'decreasing trend'. According to BirdLife International, the greatest threat to the species is occasional traditional hunting by the local Asháninka people
